text.title: API Rate Limiting Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every api-rate-limiting artefact must obey. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Limiter key MUST be derived from the AUTH-* identity (token sub / api-key id / session uid). IP-only limiting is forbidden (NAT collisions).
rule.rationale: IP-only limits punish users behind NAT/corporate proxies while leaving authenticated abuse channels open.

rule.statement: Every 429 response MUST include Retry-After header (seconds) AND RFC 7807 body with type=https://api.example.com/errors/rate-limited.
rule.rationale: Without Retry-After, well-behaved clients retry exponentially or immediately; both waste cycles.

rule.statement: Policy MUST define quotas per tier (free / paid / partner / internal). One quota for all tiers is forbidden.

rationale.source: Stripe / GitHub rate-limit guidance + Internal — quota audit

rule.rationale: Single quota either starves free tier or bankrupts on paid abuse; per-tier limits match capacity to revenue.

rule.statement: Each tier MUST set both sustained (per-minute / per-hour) and burst (per-second) limits. Single-limit policies are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Sustained-only allows traffic spikes that overload backends; burst-only allows long-running abuse below the burst threshold.

rule.statement: Policy MUST emit metrics: limit hits / tier, rejected requests / tier, p95 limiter latency. Silent limiting is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent rate-limit causes mysterious 429s in production; metrics let SRE see the policy working (or misfiring).
